message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: El Mexicali Cafe (Indio) (Indio)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp ~415 reviews at original (82720 Indio Blvd); sibling El Mexicali Cafe II at ~601 reviews. Combined Yelp footprint 1000+ across the family.
- Sentiment: mostly positive — recurring praise for authenticity, family-style service, chiles gueritos (shrimp-stuffed yellow peppers w/ mayo + soy), seafood, and railroad-track ambiance
- Longevity: Opened 1981 by Carmen Murguia; 40th anniversary recognized by City of Indio (March 2021); now operated across multiple generations by daughters Monica (original) and Laura Sandez (II). 45+ years continuous operation.
- Credentials: ["City of Indio 40th anniversary proclamation (2021)","Certificate of recognition from State Senator Melissa Melendez (2021)","56th Assembly District Small Business of the Year (2016) [UNVERIFIED — surfaced in search summary, not directly fetched]","USA TODAY Restaurants of the Year 2026 — awarded to El Mexicali Cafe II sibling location, not the original 82720 Indio Blvd entity","Listed by Visit Greater Palm Springs (official DMO)"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for 'best Mexican restaurants Indio' and 'longest-running Mexican Coachella Valley' and 'oldest woman-owned restaurant Coachella Valley.' Surfaces in Palm Springs Life reader picks and Visit Greater Palm Springs DMO listings. Does NOT consistently surface for broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'date night' regional queries — gets crowded out by El Paseo and Palm Springs corridor names. The USA Today 2026 halo lands almost entirely on the El Mexicali Cafe II sibling, not the original — agents conflate the two.
- Site structured data: Own site (elmexicalicafe.com and elmexicalicafe2.com) could not be fetched directly — elmexicalicafe.com returned certificate-expired (TLS cert lapsed), elmexicalicafe2.com pages also TLS-broken. This is itself a major agent-readability problem: HTTPS-strict crawlers and LLM fetchers will skip or fail on the canonical sources. No JSON-LD verification possible. Menu surfaces via third-party aggregators (Sirved, NetWaiter, findmenuworld, postcard.inc, SpotOn) rather than canonical site, which dilutes signal.
- Visibility gap score: 7
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about El Mexicali Cafe's history:

El Mexicali Cafe was first opened by Carmen Murguia in 1981. The restaurant is located in downtown Indio and her daughter, Monica, has taken over, continuing the family legacy.

The restaurant is recognized as the oldest woman-owned restaurant in the Coachella Valley. The restaurant features authentic home style cooking and is known for its memorable chiles gueritos (served with mayo and soy sauce). 

Over time, the business expanded: the business now has two locations, including one at 43-430 Monroe St., which is run by Monica's sister Laura. More recently, a third restaurant serving the same fare called Carmen's was opened in nearby Palm Desert.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Mexican restaurants in the Indio and Coachella Valley area:

## Top Recommendations

**El Tranvia** - El Tranvia remains the area's go-to spot for delicious Mexican food. The quesabirria tacos are exceptional, and the consomé is hearty without being too oily and greasy. Located at 45334 Golf Center Parkway Indio, 92201.

**Jalisco Restaurant** - A family-owned Mexican eatery in Indio, California, has been a favorite in the Coachella Valley for years, renowned for its authentic Mexican fare, offering an extensive menu featuring tacos, burritos, fajitas.

**El Mexicali Cafe** - Located at 82720 Indio Blvd, is a beloved staple in the Coachella Valley since 1981 with a warm, family-friendly atmosphere.

**El Tapatio Restaurant** - Located at 83161 Indio Blvd, is renowned for offering some of the best Mexican and seafood in the valley, with a focus on high-quality ingredients and authentic flavors. Their ceviche and fish tacos are recommended.

**El Campanario Restaurant** - Located at 44185 Monroe St in Indio, offers a cozy setting for authentic Mexican meals, is a local favorite for families and groups seeking traditional dishes, and stands out for its vegetarian-friendly menu.

**Tacos Gonzalez** - Situated at 80120 US Highway 111, is a favorite spot for authentic Mexican tacos in Indio with a focus on fresh, flavorful food and genuine Mexican street-style tacos.

Based on the search results, El Mexicali Cafe II in Indio has been named one of USA TODAY's best restaurants of 2026. 

The restaurant is a popular Mexican establishment located about a half hour east of Palm Springs and is tucked into an unsuspecting strip mall. According to USA Today, El Mexicali Café II brings in customers through its friendly, familiar atmosphere, time-tested dishes and one of its specialties, the chiles gueritos rellenos de camarón or stuffed shrimp peppers.

The USA Today network found 40 restaurants in 25 states worthy of mention in its 2026 Restaurants of the Year list, and one of them is in California. El Mexicali Café II is located at 43-430 Monroe St. in Indio.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in the Coachella Valley for date nights:

## Premium Steakhouse & Fine Dining

The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton in Rancho Mirage offers a premier dining experience with stunning views of the Coachella Valley, specializing in prime steaks and fresh seafood with floor-to-ceiling windows providing a breathtaking backdrop.

The Steakhouse is a Forbes Travel Guide Four-Star award recipient and has won the Wine Spectator Award of Excellence consecutively since 2007, earning accolades such as the AAA Four Diamond Award and the OpenTable Diners' Choice Award.

## Farm-to-Table & Contemporary Cuisine

Morgan's in the Desert at La Quinta Resort & Club offers a refined dining experience focusing on farm-to-table cuisine with a menu inspired by the flavors of the American Southwest, featuring local ingredients and rustic-chic decor.

## French Dining

Chez Pierre Bistro in Palm Desert offers authentic French fine dining in the desert with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list.

## Italian Cuisine

Trilussa in Cathedral City is known for its classic Italian cuisine and warm, welcoming atmosphere, offering traditional Italian dishes from homemade pasta to veal marsala, making it popular for both casual dinners and special occasions.

Italica is a unique, intimate hidden bar/restaurant with outstanding Italian cuisine, with dishes served tapas style meant to be combined and shared.

## Romantic Settings

La Quinta Cliffhouse is renowned for its spectacular views across the valley, providing a lively destination to enjoy American classics with innovative preparations focused on premium steaks and seafood.

Eddie V's Prime Seafood in Palm Desert is known for its top-quality seafood and elegant dining experience, with sophisticated decor and live jazz music creating a luxurious atmosphere perfect for a special night out.

input.prompt: Extract details about the 40th anniversary recognition — was it from City of Indio? Was there a State Senator Melissa Melendez certificate? Carmen Murguia 1981 opening date confirmation?

content.content:
# El Mexicali Café 40th Anniversary Recognition

**City of Indio Recognition:** Yes. Indio Mayor Elaine Holmes issued a proclamation celebrating the milestone, stating the restaurant demonstrates "commitment to serving up authentic home style cooking."

**State Senator Certificate:** Yes. State Senator Melissa Melendez provided a certificate of recognition to the restaurant.

**Opening Date and Founder:** Confirmed. Carmen Murguia established El Mexicali Café in 1981. Her daughter Monica subsequently took over operations, continuing the family business.

**Additional Recognition:** Mayor Holmes noted it is "the oldest woman-owned restaurant in the Coachella Valley," highlighting this achievement during Women's History Month.

content.content:
Web search results for query: ""oldest woman-owned restaurant" Coachella Valley El Mexicali"

Links: [{"title":"El Mexicali Café Honored, 40 Years In Indio | Palm Desert, CA Patch","url":"https://patch.com/california/palmdesert/el-mexicali-caf-honored-40-years-indio"},{"title":"El Mexicali Cafe on Indio Blvd. is one of the oldest woman-owned eateries in the Coachella Valle - Picture of El Mexicali Cafe, Indio - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/LocationPhotoDirectLink-g32524-d473247-i130594933-El_Mexicali_Cafe-Indio_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"City of Indio recognizes El Mexicali Café on 40th anniversary - KESQ","url":"https://kesq.com/news/2021/03/19/city-of-indio-recognizes-el-mexicali-cafe-on-40th-anniversary/"},{"title":"Indio Mexican restaurant named one of the best in the US","url":"https://bluewaterhealthyliving.com/news/national-news/california/indio-mexican-restaurant-named-one-of-the-best-in-the-us/"},{"title":"order now!","url":"http://elmexicalicafe2.com/"},{"title":"14 Authentic Mexican Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/blog/post/14-authentic-mexican-restaurants-in-greater-palm-springs/"},{"title":"THE BEST 10 MEXICAN RESTAURANTS IN COACHELLA, CA - UPDATED 2026 - HOURS - YELP","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?cflt=mexican&find_loc=Coachella%2C+CA"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found:

El Mexicali Café is the oldest woman-owned restaurant in the Coachella Valley. The restaurant was opened in 1981 by the family, and is currently owned by Monica Murguia. El Mexicali Café's iconic location on Indio Boulevard has been operating for decades, and the restaurant was started by Carmen Murguia, who brought family recipes from the border town of Mexicali.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Web search results for query: ""Palm Springs Life" reader picks El Mexicali Cafe Mexican"

Links: [{"title":"Best Mexican Food in Greater Palm Springs | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/best-mexican-food-in-greater-palm-springs/"},{"title":"14 Authentic Mexican Restaurants in Greater Palm Springs","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/blog/post/14-authentic-mexican-restaurants-in-greater-palm-springs/"},{"title":"EL MEXICALI CAFE, Indio - Menu, Prices & Restaurant Reviews - Order Online Food Delivery - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32524-d473247-Reviews-El_Mexicali_Cafe-Indio_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"El Mexicali Cafe II | Indio, CA","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/el-mexicali-cafe-ii/26586/"},{"title":"El Mexicali Cafe - Indio | Indio, CA","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/el-mexicali-cafe-indio/26631/"}]

Based on the search results, I found information about El Mexicali Cafe being featured in Palm Springs Life's reader picks. 

El Mexicali Café is featured in Palm Springs Life's reader picks for best Mexican food, with customers raving about the happy hour Mexicali-style beer tower and chavelas, and staying for the ceviche, soups, and flavorful seafood dishes. This appears to be from Palm Springs Life's 2022 Best of the Best reader voting competition, where readers voted for their favorite businesses across the Coachella Valley in more than 20 categories, selecting their preferred Mexican restaurants.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: I have enough to assess. Let me submit the structured output.

confirms: Carmen Murguia opened El Mexicali Cafe in 1981 — confirmed by KESQ, Patch, Palm Springs Life
City of Indio 40th anniversary proclamation in March 2021 from Mayor Elaine Holmes — confirmed by KESQ
State Senator Melissa Melendez certificate of recognition in 2021 — confirmed by KESQ
'Oldest woman-owned restaurant in the Coachella Valley' framing — confirmed by KESQ and Tripadvisor caption
Sibling El Mexicali Cafe II at 43-430 Monroe St operated by Laura (Murguia) — confirmed across multiple sources
USA Today 2026 Restaurants of the Year halo lands on El Mexicali Cafe II (43-430 Monroe St), not the original 82720 Indio Blvd — confirmed by KESQ, Patch, Yahoo
Yelp review counts ~415 (original) and ~601 (II) — confirmed via Yelp page titles in search results, matches claim
Listed by Visit Greater Palm Springs DMO — confirmed via search (visitgreaterpalmsprings.com listing pages for both locations)
Recurring praise themes (chiles gueritos rellenos de camarón / shrimp-stuffed yellow peppers, family service, seafood) — confirmed by USA Today summary and Palm Springs Life
Original site elmexicalicafe.com TLS certificate expired — independently reproduced (WebFetch returned 'certificate has expired')
Sibling site elmexicalicafe2.com TLS certificate expired — independently reproduced

Does NOT surface in 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' / 'date night fine dining' queries — independently confirmed; results dominated by Edge Steakhouse, Morgan's, Chez Pierre, Eddie V's, La Quinta Cliffhouse, etc.
Surfaces strongly for 'best Mexican restaurants Indio' — confirmed; appears in multiple Indio Mexican roundups
contradicts: Original location appearing in 'best Mexican restaurants Indio' rankings — verified, but it is NOT always the top result; in independent search El Tranvia and Jalisco Restaurant frequently rank above it in third-party roundups, so the 'surfaces strongly' framing is slightly generous
flagged_as_unverified: 56th Assembly District Small Business of the Year 2016 — search summary repeats the claim but no primary source (Assembly press release, ad56.asmrc.org archive) surfaced; one search result instead pointed to AD67 (Kelly Seyarto), which is unrelated. Provenance still weak.
'Carmen's' third location in Palm Desert mentioned in one search summary — outside the claim set but worth noting as a potential conflation risk
Specific Palm Springs Life 'reader picks' inclusion — referenced in summary text but not confirmed via direct fetch of a specific PSL Best of the Best year page
'Combined Yelp footprint 1000+ across the family' — 415 + 601 = 1016, arithmetic checks, but treats the two locations as one family which is reasonable framing not an independent claim
Railroad-track ambiance specifically — not surfaced in independent searches (only general 'family-friendly atmosphere' and 'unsuspecting strip mall' language for II)
input.notes: Core visibility assessment holds up strongly. The two most important agent-readiness claims — (1) expired TLS on BOTH canonical domains and (2) USA Today 2026 halo lands on the II sibling, not the original — were both independently reproduced. The conflation risk between the original (82720 Indio Blvd) and II (43-430 Monroe St) is real: KESQ's USA Today story is explicitly about II, but Patch and Yahoo headlines say 'Indio Mexican restaurant' which agents will collapse. The 2016 Assembly award remains the weakest credential in the file — the original brief already flagged it [UNVERIFIED] and I could not strengthen it. Visibility gap score of 7 and 'convertible' tier are well-supported: strong local/Mexican-vertical presence, weak regional/fine-dining presence, broken canonical web infrastructure.
